hero.headline: Source-checked Roblox codes and game guides.
hero.subheadline: Quest Codes tracks working codes, expired history, class notes, crafting routes, and update checks without inventing game data.

features.title: Built for Roblox players who need the answer now
features.description: Short, source-aware pages for codes, routes, rewards, and update checks.

auth.title: Working codes
auth.description: Active, special, and expired code tables stay separated so players know what to test first.

payment.title: Source trail
payment.description: Each code page keeps a visible source trail and flags conflicts instead of hiding them.

rbac.title: Guide clusters
rbac.description: Related pages connect codes, crafting, classes, gems, maps, badges, and survival routes.

i18n.title: Update checks
i18n.description: The updates page records source checks and avoids turning rumors into patch notes.

cms.title: Player intent
cms.description: Pages are built around real search intent: how to redeem, what to craft, where to go, and what to avoid.

credits.title: Conservative data
credits.description: If a reward, drop rate, or status cannot be checked, it stays out of the published facts.

pricing.title: Support Quest Codes
pricing.description: Quest Codes is currently free to use while the guide library grows.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: How Quest Codes handles game data and Roblox guide updates.

stack.question: Is Quest Codes affiliated with Roblox?
stack.answer: No. Quest Codes is a fan-made guide site and is not affiliated with Roblox or individual game developers.

payment.question: How are active codes checked?
payment.answer: We compare trusted public source pages, keep checked dates visible, and flag conflicts when sources disagree.

database.question: Why not publish every rumored code?
database.answer: Wrong game data wastes player time. Rumors, blocked sources, and unconfirmed reward amounts stay out of the main facts until they can be reviewed.

customize.question: Which game is the first focus?
customize.answer: The first content cluster focuses on 99 Nights in the Forest because it has active search demand across codes, crafting, classes, gems, badges, and routes.

license.question: Will more games be added?
license.answer: Yes, but only when keyword demand and reliable source material justify a full guide cluster instead of a thin page.

cta.headline: Start with the latest checked guides.
cta.subheadline: Browse source-aware Roblox code pages and route guides before your next run.

footer.tagline: Source-checked Roblox routes, codes, and update notes.
